title: Wholesome Jamaican Kombu Stew
description: A sour, tender, one-pot Jamaican stew bursting with umami kombu and sweet vegetables, perfect for a lacto-vegetarian meal.
instructions: Rinse kombu and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a pot over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until fragrant and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Stir in diced sweet potato and sliced carrot, letting them soften for 3 minutes.
Add the sliced dried mushrooms and curry powder, stirring until the mushrooms release moisture and the spices are toasted.
Pour in two cups of vegetable stock, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, and let it cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the kombu pieces and coconut milk, allowing the stew to simmer for another 15 minutes until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Season with salt, stirring to combine the flavors evenly.
Reduce heat to low and let the stew thicken for an additional 5 minutes, stirring gently.
Serve hot in a talavera plate, garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh herbs if desired.
